sentence: Vos sabés que el zorro azul corre por el campo.
ipa: bos saβes ke el soro asul kore poɾ el kampo
gloss_en: You know the blue fox runs across the field.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. voseo verbal morphology (sabés) and Guatemalan usage per hualde2005.

sentence: Tomá la taza de agua y vení a la milpa.
ipa: toma la tasa de aɡa i beni a la milpa
gloss_en: Take the cup of water and come to the maize field.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. voseo imperatives (tomá, vení) and Guatemalan 'milpa' per hualde2005.

sentence: El patojo tiene un perro grande y bravo.
ipa: el patoxo tjene un pero ɡɾande i bɾabo
gloss_en: The kid has a big, fierce dog.
notes: tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. Guatemalan 'patojo' for a child per hualde2005.

sentence: Mañana el niño juega en la plaza con vos.
ipa: maɲana el niɲo xweɣa en la plasa kon bos
gloss_en: Tomorrow the boy plays in the square with you.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. voseo object pronoun (con vos) per hualde2005.

sentence: La calle está llena de gente joven.
ipa: la kaʝe esta ʝena de xente xoben
gloss_en: The street is full of young people.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. general Central American usage per hualde2005.

sentence: ¿Querés arroz con pollo o preferís frijoles?
ipa: keɾes aros kon poʝo o pɾefeɾis fɾixoles
gloss_en: Do you want rice with chicken or do you prefer beans?
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. voseo interrogative (querés, preferís) per hualde2005.

sentence: Buscá un zapato nuevo en aquel mercado.
ipa: buska un sapato nwebo en akel meɾkaðo
gloss_en: Look for a new shoe in that market.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. voseo imperative (buscá) per hualde2005.

sentence: La lluvia cae fuerte sobre el tejado viejo.
ipa: la ʝubja kae fweɾte sobɾe el texaðo bjexo
gloss_en: The rain falls hard on the old roof.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. general Guatemalan usage per hualde2005.

sentence: Nosotros queremos una casa buena y barata.
ipa: nosotɾos keɾemos una kasa bwena i baɾata
gloss_en: We want a good, cheap house.
notes: tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: El carro rojo corre rápido por la carretera.
ipa: el karo roxo kore rapiðo poɾ la kareteɾa
gloss_en: The red car runs fast along the highway.
notes: strong-R trill [r] per lipski1994. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: Ese señor tiene mucha tierra en la montaña.
ipa: ese seɲoɾ tjene mutʃa tjera en la montaɲa
gloss_en: That gentleman has a lot of land on the mountain.
notes: palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: No vayás solo, andá con tu compañero.
ipa: no baʝas solo anda kon tu kompaɲeɾo
gloss_en: Don't go alone, go with your companion.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. voseo negative imperative (vayás, andá) per hualde2005.

sentence: La manzana y la naranja están en la mesa.
ipa: la mansana i la naɾanxa estan en la mesa
gloss_en: The apple and the orange are on the table.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: ¿Vos vivís lejos del parque nuevo?
ipa: bos bibis lexos del paɾke nwebo
gloss_en: Do you live far from the new park?
notes: tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. voseo (vivís) per hualde2005.

sentence: El caballo blanco pasta junto al arroyo.
ipa: el kaβaʝo blanko pasta xunto al aroʝo
gloss_en: The white horse grazes next to the stream.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: Comprá pan y guardá un poco para luego.
ipa: kompɾa pan i ɡaɾda un poko paɾa lweɣo
gloss_en: Buy bread and save some for later.
notes: tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. voseo imperatives (comprá, guardá) per hualde2005.

sentence: Los zapatos viejos ya no sirven para nada.
ipa: los sapatos bjexos ʝa no siɾben paɾa naða
gloss_en: The old shoes are no good for anything anymore.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: Aquella niña baila con mucha alegría.
ipa: akeʝa niɲa bajla kon mutʃa aleɡɾia
gloss_en: That girl dances with great joy.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: Tenés que llamar a tu mamá temprano.
ipa: tenes ke ʝamaɾ a tu mama tempɾano
gloss_en: You have to call your mom early.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ] per lipski1994. voseo (tenés) per hualde2005.

sentence: El sol brilla sobre el mar azul y tranquilo.
ipa: el sol bɾiʝa sobɾe el maɾ asul i tɾankilo
gloss_en: The sun shines over the blue, calm sea.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; cross-word sandhi junction per lipski1994. general usage per hualde2005.
